title: Config
Config

projects.description: List of projects operator can assign work to

sessions.description: Session wrapper configuration (tmux, vscode, or cmux)

kanban.description: Kanban provider configuration for syncing issues from Jira, Linear, etc.

version_check.description: Version check configuration for automatic update notifications

delegators.description: Agent delegator configurations for autonomous ticket launching

model_servers.description:
User-declared model servers (ollama, lmstudio, any OpenAI-compat host).
Implicit builtin servers exist for each `llm_tool`'s vendor API and do not need declaration.

max_agents_per_repo.description:
Maximum concurrent agents per project/repo (default: 1).
Requires `git.use_worktrees` = true when > 1 to avoid conflicts.

generation_timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for each agent generation (default: 300 = 5 min)

sync_interval.description: Interval in seconds between ticket-session syncs (default: 60)

step_timeout.description: Maximum seconds a step can run before timing out (default: 1800 = 30 min)

silence_threshold.description: Seconds of tmux silence before considering agent awaiting input (default: 30)

NotificationsConfig.description: Notifications configuration with support for multiple integrations.

enabled.description: Global enabled flag for all notifications

events.description:
Events to send (empty = all events)
Possible values: agent.started, agent.completed, agent.failed,
`agent.awaiting_input`, `agent.session_lost`, pr.created, pr.merged,
pr.closed, `pr.ready_to_merge`, `pr.changes_requested`,
ticket.returned, investigation.created

name.description: Optional name for this webhook (for logging)

token_env.description: Environment variable containing the bearer token

password_env.description: Environment variable containing the password for basic auth

DockerConfig.description: Docker execution configuration for running agents in containers

enabled.description: Whether docker mode option is available in launch dialog

image.description: Docker image to use (required if enabled)

mount_path.description: Container mount path for the project (default: /workspace)

env_vars.description: Environment variables to pass through to the container

YoloConfig.description: YOLO (auto-accept) mode configuration for fully autonomous execution

enabled.description: Whether YOLO mode option is available in launch dialog

preset.description:
Named preset for issue type collection
Options: simple, `dev_kanban`, `devops_kanban`, custom

collection.description:
Custom issuetype collection (only used when preset = custom)
List of issue type keys: TASK, FEAT, FIX, SPIKE, INV

active_collection.description:
Active collection name (overrides preset if set)
Can be a builtin preset name or a user-defined collection

oneOf.description: DevOps kanban: TASK, SPIKE, INV, FEAT, FIX

pr_check_interval_secs.description: Interval in seconds between PR status checks (default: 60)

rate_limit_check_interval_secs.description: Interval in seconds between rate limit checks (default: 300)

rate_limit_warning_threshold.description: Show warning when rate limit remaining is below this percentage (default: 0.2)

level.description: Log level filter (trace, debug, info, warn, error)

to_file.description: Whether to log to file in TUI mode (false = stderr for debugging)

config_generated.description: Whether custom tmux config has been generated

SessionsConfig.description:
Session wrapper configuration

Controls how operator creates and manages terminal sessions for agents.
Four modes are supported:
- tmux: Standalone tmux sessions (default)
- vscode: VS Code integrated terminal (requires extension)
- cmux: macOS terminal multiplexer (requires running inside cmux)
- zellij: Zellij terminal workspace manager

SessionWrapperType.description: Session wrapper type for terminal session management

oneOf.description: VS Code integrated terminal (via extension webhook)

config_generated.description: Whether custom tmux config has been generated

SessionsCmuxConfig.description: cmux macOS terminal multiplexer session configuration

require_in_cmux.description: Require running inside cmux (`CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID` env var present)

placement.description: Where to place new agent sessions: "auto", "workspace", or "window"

CmuxPlacementPolicy.description: Placement policy for cmux sessions: where to create new agent terminals

oneOf.description: Automatically choose: 0-1 windows → new workspace, >1 windows → new window
Always create a new workspace in the active window
Always create a new window for each ticket

SessionsZellijConfig.description: Zellij terminal workspace manager session configuration

require_in_zellij.description: Require running inside Zellij (ZELLIJ env var present)

detected.description: Detected CLI tools (populated on first startup)

providers.description:
Available {tool, model} pairs for launching tickets
Built from detected tools + their model aliases

skill_directory_overrides.description: Per-tool overrides for skill directories (keyed by `tool_name`)

DetectedTool.description: A detected CLI tool (e.g., claude binary)

min_version.description: Minimum required version for Operator compatibility

version_ok.description: Whether the installed version meets the minimum requirement

supports_sessions.description: Whether the tool supports session continuity via UUID

supports_headless.description: Whether the tool can run in headless/non-interactive mode

LlmProvider.description:
A {tool, model} pair that can be selected when launching tickets.
Includes optional variant fields adopted from vibe-kanban's profile system.

display_name.description: Optional display name for UI (e.g., "Claude Opus", "Codex High")

flags.description: Additional CLI flags for this provider (e.g., ["--dangerously-skip-permissions"])

env.description: Environment variables to set when launching

approvals.description: Whether this provider requires approval gates

plan_only.description: Whether to run in plan-only mode

enabled.description: Whether the REST API is enabled

port.description: Port for the REST API server

GitConfig.description: Git provider configuration for PR/MR operations

provider.description: Active provider (auto-detected from remote URL if not specified)

use_worktrees.description:
Whether to use git worktrees for per-ticket isolation (default: false)
When false, tickets work directly in the project directory with branches

token_env.description: Environment variable containing the GitHub token (default: `GITHUB_TOKEN`)
Environment variable containing the GitLab token (default: `GITLAB_TOKEN`)

host.description: GitLab host (default: gitlab.com, can be self-hosted)

KanbanConfig.description:
Kanban provider configuration for syncing issues from external systems

Providers are keyed by domain/workspace:
- Jira: keyed by domain (e.g., "foobar.atlassian.net")
- Linear: keyed by workspace slug (e.g., "myworkspace")
- GitHub Projects: keyed by owner login (e.g., "my-org")

jira.description: Jira Cloud instances keyed by domain (e.g., "foobar.atlassian.net")

linear.description: Linear instances keyed by workspace slug

github.description:
GitHub Projects v2 instances keyed by owner login (user or org)

NOTE: This is the *kanban* GitHub integration (Projects v2), distinct
from `GitHubConfig` which is the *git provider* used for PRs and
branches. The two use different env vars and different scopes — see
`docs/getting-started/kanban/github.md` for the full disambiguation.

JiraConfig.description:
Jira Cloud provider configuration

The domain is specified as the `HashMap` key in KanbanConfig.jira

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name containing the API key (default: `OPERATOR_JIRA_API_KEY`)

ProjectSyncConfig.description: Per-project/team sync configuration for a kanban provider

sync_user_id.description:
User ID to sync issues for (provider-specific format)
- Jira: accountId (e.g., "5e3f7acd9876543210abcdef")
- Linear: user ID (e.g., "abc12345-6789-0abc-def0-123456789abc")
- GitHub Projects: numeric GitHub `databaseId` (e.g., "12345678")

sync_statuses.description: Workflow statuses to sync (empty = default/first status only)

collection_name.description:
Optional `IssueTypeCollection` name this project maps to.
Not required for kanban onboarding or sync.

type_mappings.description:
Explicit mapping: kanban issue type ID → operator issue type key (e.g., TASK, FEAT, FIX).
Multiple kanban types can map to the same operator template.

bidirectional.description:
When true, operator pushes status changes and activity logs back to this kanban project.
Ticket state changes (todo→doing, doing→done) and step completions with delegator info
are reflected upstream. Default: false.

LinearConfig.description:
Linear provider configuration

The workspace slug is specified as the `HashMap` key in KanbanConfig.linear

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name containing the API key (default: `OPERATOR_LINEAR_API_KEY`)

GithubProjectsConfig.description:
GitHub Projects v2 (kanban) provider configuration

The owner login (user or org) is specified as the `HashMap` key in
`KanbanConfig.github`. Project keys inside `projects` are `GraphQL` node
IDs (e.g., `PVT_kwDOABcdefg`) — opaque, stable identifiers used directly
by every GitHub Projects v2 mutation without needing a lookup.

**Distinct from `GitHubConfig`** (the git provider used for PR/branch
operations). They live in different parts of the config tree, use
different env vars (`OPERATOR_GITHUB_TOKEN` vs `GITHUB_TOKEN`), and
require different OAuth scopes (`project` vs `repo`). See
`docs/getting-started/kanban/github.md` for the full rationale.

api_key_env.description:
Environment variable name containing the GitHub token (default:
`OPERATOR_GITHUB_TOKEN`). The token must have `project` (or
`read:project`) scope, NOT just `repo` — see the disambiguation
guide in the kanban github docs.

projects.description: Per-project sync configuration. Keys are `GraphQL` project node IDs.

VersionCheckConfig.description: Version check configuration for automatic update notifications

enabled.description: Enable automatic version checking on startup

url.description: URL to fetch latest version from (optional, can be removed)

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for version check HTTP request

Delegator.description:
Agent delegator configuration for autonomous ticket launching

A delegator is a named {tool, model} pairing with optional launch configuration
that can be used to launch agents for tickets.

llm_tool.description: LLM tool name (must match a detected tool, e.g., "claude", "codex")

model_server.description:
Name of a declared `ModelServer` (from `Config.model_servers`).
`None` means use the `llm_tool`'s implicit vendor default
(claude → anthropic-api, codex → openai-api, gemini → google-api).

DelegatorLaunchConfig.description:
Launch configuration for a delegator

Controls how the delegator launches agents. Optional fields use tri-state
semantics: `None` = inherit from global config, `Some(true/false)` = override.

use_worktrees.description: Override global `git.use_worktrees` per-delegator (None = use global setting)

create_branch.description: Whether to create a git branch for the ticket (None = default behavior)

docker.description: Run in docker container (None = use global `launch.docker.enabled`)

prompt_prefix.description: Prompt text to prepend before the generated step prompt

prompt_suffix.description: Prompt text to append after the generated step prompt

operator_relay.description: Override global relay auto-inject MCP setting per-delegator (None = use global setting)

ModelServer.description:
A named host that serves models via an inference API.

Model servers are orthogonal to `llm_tools`: a delegator pairs an agentic CLI
(`llm_tool`, e.g. claude/codex/gemini) with a model-serving endpoint
(`model_server`, e.g. ollama-local, openai-api, a custom vllm host).

Implicit builtin servers (`anthropic-api`, `openai-api`, `google-api`) are
returned by [`implicit_model_server_for_tool`] and do not need to be declared
in config.

base_url.description:
Base URL of the inference endpoint (e.g., `http://localhost:11434`).
`None` for implicit vendor servers means use the SDK default.

api_key_env.description: Name of an env var providing the API key (e.g., `OLLAMA_API_KEY`)

extra_env.description: Additional environment variables set when spawning agents that use this server

auto_inject_mcp.description:
When true, automatically inject the relay MCP server for all delegators.
When false (default), relay injection is opt-in per delegator.

http_enabled.description:
Whether to mount MCP HTTP/SSE endpoints on the REST API server.
Toggling requires an API restart (no hot-swap of the axum router).

stdio_advertised.description:
Whether the descriptor endpoint advertises the `operator mcp` stdio
command. Set to false on multi-tenant/remote deployments where clients
shouldn't spawn local subprocesses.

expose_ticket_write_tools.description:
Whether to expose ticket-mutating tools (claim, complete, return-to-queue,
create) over MCP. Defaults to `false` because any MCP client can call them.

external_servers.description:
External MCP servers to inject into spawned agent sessions.
Each entry produces a separate `--mcp-config` file alongside the
relay config when launching Claude Code agents.

ExternalMcpServer.description:
An external MCP server to inject into spawned agent sessions.

Values in `command`, `args`, and `env` support `${VAR}` interpolation,
expanded at spawn time from the operator process environment.

When `discover_from` is set, operator reads an MCP server spec from that
JSON sidecar file at spawn time. The sidecar must contain a top-level
`mcpServer` object with `command`, `args`, and `env` fields. If the file
is absent and `command` is empty, the server is silently skipped.

name.description:
Server name used as the key in the `mcpServers` JSON object
(e.g., "kanbots"). Must be unique across all external servers.

args.description: Command arguments. Each element supports `${VAR}` interpolation.

env.description:
Environment variables passed to the MCP server process.
Values support `${VAR}` interpolation.

enabled.description: Whether this server is enabled. Allows disabling without removing config.

discover_from.description:
Path to a JSON sidecar discovery file. Relative paths resolve from
the project directory. The sidecar must contain `{ "mcpServer": { ... } }`.
When the file exists, its `mcpServer` spec is used verbatim (overriding
`command`/`args`/`env`). When absent and `command` is empty, the server
is silently skipped.

AcpConfig.description:
Agent Client Protocol (ACP) agent configuration.

Operator runs as an ACP agent over stdio when editors (Zed, `JetBrains`,
Emacs `agent-shell`, Kiro, etc.) spawn `operator acp`. Each ACP session
maps to an in-progress ACP ticket and a delegator subprocess.

stdio_advertised.description:
Whether the dashboard advertises the `operator acp` stdio entrypoint
(and editor-config snippet actions). Set to false on machines that
shouldn't be used as ACP agents.

default_delegator.description:
Name of the delegator (from `[[delegators]]`) to use for ACP prompts.
If unset or not found, falls back to the operator's default delegator
resolution.

max_concurrent_sessions.description:
Maximum number of concurrent ACP sessions. New `session/new` requests
beyond this limit are rejected with a JSON-RPC error.
